note: RQ2 ablation, real-bytecode, 240 runs/variant, 2026-06-21. DR saturates 12/12; coverage discriminates.

-SE.note: detections are hollow generic-invariant trips; xcc 2.6%, 0 basic blocks executed

-Sync.differential: violated-invariant SET identical to synced on all 240 matched seed-pairs (0 lost, 0 added); only raw violation COUNTS shift per-bridge (nomad 120 vs 140, fegtoken 65 vs 81 down; pgala 259 vs 242, multichain 307 vs 300 up; net ~negligible)
-Sync.finding: Unlike -SE/-RAG (coverage collapse to 0 BB / 2.6% XCC = hollow), -Sync KEEPS full coverage (100% XCC, ~927 BB) and the SAME violated-invariant set; DR stays 12/12. Removing synchronization does not change detection on reconstructed benchmarks (consistent with DR-saturation). Its theoretical FPR-prevention role (spurious cross-chain states) cannot be isolated here without FPR ground-truth labeling — flagged as limitation/future work.
